questions.question: Why did South Africa need a new constitution after apartheid?
questions.answer: To establish equality and democratic governance after racial discrimination.

questions.question: What was the main aim of the African National Congress?
questions.answer: To end apartheid and establish democratic rights in South Africa.

questions.question: Why did black leaders in South Africa forgive the white rulers?
questions.answer: To build a peaceful and democratic society based on equality.

questions.question: What does the constitution specify about the government?
questions.answer: It specifies how the government will be formed and how power will be exercised.

questions.question: Why does a constitution limit government power?
questions.answer: To protect the rights and freedoms of citizens.

questions.question: What does the constitution express about society?
questions.answer: It expresses the aspirations and ideals of the people.

questions.question: Why was making the Indian Constitution a difficult task?
questions.answer: Because India was a large, diverse country facing partition and integration challenges.

questions.question: What was the role of the Drafting Committee?
questions.answer: To prepare the draft of the Indian Constitution.

questions.question: How were members of the Constituent Assembly chosen?
questions.answer: They were elected by members of provincial legislative assemblies.

questions.question: Why is the Indian Constitution considered legitimate?
questions.answer: Because it represented a broad consensus among different groups of people.

questions.question: What is the significance of the Preamble?
questions.answer: It states the philosophy and goals of the Constitution.

questions.question: What does the term 'Socialist' imply in the Constitution?
questions.answer: It implies reducing economic inequalities in society.

questions.question: What does 'Democratic' mean in the Constitution?
questions.answer: People elect their representatives and participate in governance.

questions.question: Why is fraternity important in a democracy?
questions.answer: It promotes unity and brotherhood among citizens.

questions.question: What role does the constitution play in resolving conflicts?
questions.answer: It provides rules and principles to manage disagreements peacefully.

questions.question: Why do democratic countries require written constitutions?
questions.answer: To clearly define laws, powers and rights.

questions.question: What is universal adult franchise?
questions.answer: The right of all adult citizens to vote.

questions.question: What does liberty guarantee to citizens?
questions.answer: Freedom of thought, expression and belief.

questions.question: Why is equality important in a democracy?
questions.answer: It ensures that all citizens are treated equally before the law.

questions.question: How did freedom struggle influence the Indian Constitution?
questions.answer: It shaped the democratic values and ideals of the Constitution.

questions.question: What is the meaning of constitutional amendment?
questions.answer: A change made to the constitution.

questions.question: Why should constitutional rules be difficult to change?
questions.answer: To maintain stability and protect democratic principles.

questions.question: What is meant by majority rule?
questions.answer: Decisions are made according to the preference of the majority.

questions.question: What role do citizens play in a democracy?
questions.answer: They elect representatives and participate in governance.

questions.question: What is the significance of Republic Day?
questions.answer: It marks the day when the Constitution came into force.
